01_initial_request.input_data: High-confidence PPI datasets from Drosophila and human, AlphaFold-Multimer (AFM) predictions, and Predicted Aligned Error (PAE) data
01_initial_request.user_intent: To improve the detection of direct protein-protein interactions, particularly those with small interfaces or flexible regions, by developing a new Local Interaction Score (LIS) to overcome limitations of the AFM ipTM metric
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Prediction of over 5,000 direct PPIs after evaluating more than 30,000 potential interactions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] High-confidence PPI datasets are required to train and validate computational prediction models. [Gap] Existing datasets needed to be curated into Positive Reference Sets (PRS) and Random Reference Sets (RRS) for Drosophila, human, and yeast to ensure unbiased evaluation. [Decision] To achieve reliable benchmarking, chose to use Database Curation to perform reference set preparation on UniProt and FlyBase sequences, expecting to obtain standardized PRS and RRS datasets.

parameters.sets: fly PRS, fly RRS, human PRS, human RRS, yeast PRS, yeast RRS

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Curated PPI reference sets for fly, human, and yeast were utilized, with random reference sets treated as negative controls.

references.citation: Giot et al., 2003; Yu et al., 2008; Braun et al., 2009; Tang et al., 2023
references.relevance: Sources for curated PPI reference sets

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Protein complex structures are needed to derive interaction metrics. [Gap] Standard AFM metrics like ipTM may miss flexible interactions. [Decision] To achieve structural predictions, chose to use LocalColabFold to perform AlphaFold-Multimer prediction on protein pairs, expecting to obtain 5 models per interaction with PAE data.

parameters.hardware: Nvidia GPUs (TeslaM40, TeslaV100, RTX6000, RTX8000, A40, A100, L40)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Generated five separate models for each interaction, each subjected to five cycles of refinement. Average and best scores were derived from these models.

references.citation: Mirdita et al., 2022; Evans et al., 2022

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Initial evaluation of AFM performance is necessary. [Gap] It is unknown if ipTM scores sufficiently distinguish true PPIs from controls in all cases. [Decision] To achieve baseline assessment, chose to use Statistical Analysis to perform ipTM score evaluation on PRS and control sets, expecting to obtain p-values and distribution curves.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: PRSs exhibited higher average and best ipTM scores compared to control sets (p-values: 1.38e-175 for average, 8.60e-122 for best), but some true PPIs had low ipTM scores.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] ipTM limitations identified for flexible/small interfaces. [Gap] A new metric is needed to capture local interaction confidence from PAE maps. [Decision] To achieve improved sensitivity, chose to use Custom Python Script to perform Local Interaction Score (LIS) calculation on PAE data, expecting to obtain LIS values ranging from 0 to 1.

parameters.method: Invert PAE values within Local Interaction Areas (LIA)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Derived LIS values where higher numbers denote stronger inferred interaction. LIS showed robust separation between PRSs and control sets.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LIS calculation requires an optimal PAE cutoff to define Local Interaction Areas. [Gap] The specific cutoff value that maximizes discrimination is unknown. [Decision] To achieve optimal thresholding, chose to use sklearn to perform Receiver operating characteristic (ROC) analysis on LIS values, expecting to obtain the PAE cutoff with highest AUC.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Found that a PAE cutoff of 12 maximizes the AUC, yielding an average LIS of 0.911 and a best LIS value of 0.891.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LIS performance needs to be benchmarked against existing metrics. [Gap] It is unclear if LIS outperforms ipTM, pDockQ, or Model Confidence. [Decision] To achieve comparative validation, chose to use Statistical Analysis to perform metric comparison (AUC, correlation heatmaps) on test sets, expecting to obtain performance rankings.

parameters.metrics: LIS, ipTM, pDockQ, pDockQ2, Model Confidence, LIA

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Average LIS scored the highest AUC. LIS exhibited the lowest correlation with pLDDT and pTM, indicating independence from structural accuracy.

references.citation: Burke et al., 2023; Zhu et al., 2023

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LIS was validated on Fly/Human sets. [Gap] Generalizability to other organisms and motif-based interactions needs testing. [Decision] To achieve broader validation, chose to use LIS Calculation to perform analysis on Yeast PRS and ELM-annotated PPIs, expecting to obtain prediction rates for direct interactions.

parameters.datasets: Yeast PRS, Yeast RRS, ELM-annotated PPIs
parameters.thresholds: Optimal average and best LIS thresholds

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Over 70% of yeast positive PPIs and ELM-annotated PPIs were predicted to be direct interactions. Substantial proportion of ELM PPIs had low ipTM but high LIS.

references.citation: Yu et al., 2008; Kumar et al., 2024

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Specific complex interactomes need detailed mapping. [Gap] IP-MS data contains indirect interactions that need filtering. [Decision] To achieve network expansion, chose to use Sequential AFM analysis to perform iterative screening on MTC IP-MS dataset, expecting to obtain a refined binary PPI network.

parameters.strategy: Bait vs Prey -> Group 1 vs Group 1 -> Group 2 vs Group 2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified Kap-alpha3 and 14-3-3 epsilon as central hubs. Progressively resolved binary interactions within the CCT complex.

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Large-scale PPI prediction is the primary goal. [Gap] High-confidence subsets of large-scale experimental datasets need identification. [Decision] To achieve the quantifiable goal, chose to use LIS Calculation to perform analysis on FlyBi, DPiM, and Literature datasets, expecting to obtain over 5,000 direct PPI predictions.

parameters.thresholds: Average and best LIS thresholds from ROC

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Predicted over 5,000 direct PPIs after evaluating more than 30,000 potential interactions. 25% of FlyBi, 16% of DPiM, and 28% of literature PPIs met thresholds.

references.citation: Tang et al., 2023; Guruharsha et al., 2011

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Researchers need access to the prediction data. [Gap] No centralized platform exists for these AFM-LIS predictions. [Decision] To achieve data dissemination, chose to use Web Framework to perform FlyPredictome database construction, expecting to obtain an online resource for PPI exploration.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Developed FlyPredictome including over 80,000 PPI predictions covering 8,643 Drosophila genes.

03_success_verification.validation_technique: ROC Analysis and Large-scale Dataset Application

AUC_LIS.interpretation: Average LIS achieved highest AUC compared to ipTM and other metrics

PAE_Cutoff.interpretation: Optimal threshold determined by ROC analysis for maximizing discriminatory power

PPIs_Predicted.interpretation: Number of direct PPIs predicted after evaluating potential interactions

PPIs_Evaluated.interpretation: Total potential interactions screened to achieve the prediction goal

FlyPredictome_Coverage.interpretation: Total PPI predictions hosted in the database covering 8,643 genes

03_success_verification.final_verdict: The research objective was successfully achieved. The study developed the Local Interaction Score (LIS) which outperformed the standard AFM ipTM metric (AUC 0.911), particularly for interactions with small interfaces or flexible regions. The application of LIS to large-scale Drosophila datasets resulted in the prediction of over 5,000 direct PPIs from more than 30,000 evaluated interactions, meeting the quantifiable goal. The FlyPredictome database was also successfully deployed to host these predictions.
